title: Fagraea peregrina
intro: Fagraea peregrina, commonly known as the strychnine tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Loganiaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in Southeast Asian and Pacific Island cultures for its medicinal properties, with potential health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, as well as its ability to treat fever, respiratory issues, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Fagraea peregrina include alkaloids, such as strychnine and other loganines, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Fagraea peregrina is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is also used in herbal remedies such as syrups and tonics.
taxonomy: Fagraea peregrina is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Gentianaceae. It is native to tropical Asia and belongs to the genus Fagraea. The plant is known for its fragrant flowers, which are white or purple in color. Fagraea peregrina is a small to medium-sized tree or shrub that is often found growing in shady areas. The plant is native to countries such as Malaysia, the Philippines, and Indonesia.
